title: Incompatibility with the "docker-py" library because /_ping is not versioned (related to #6548)
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
(This has been previously reported at #6548 but that issue is closed)

I was trying to use the [aws-sam-cli](https://github.com/awslabs/aws-sam-cli) tool when I got complained that it couldn't talk with docker.
It uses [docker-py](https://github.com/docker/docker-py) internally and ultimately calls this line
https://github.com/docker/docker-py/blob/master/docker/api/daemon.py#L166
to check whether docker is reachable or not.

However, `self._url('/_ping')` returns `'http+docker://localhost/v1.35/_ping'` which is currently not supported by podman.

You may find the following snippet useful:
```
$ podman system service --time=0 &
$ export DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
$ python
Python 3.8.3 (default, May 29 2020, 00:00:00) 
[GCC 10.1.1 20200507 (Red Hat 10.1.1-1)] on linux
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import docker
>>> client = docker.from_env()
>>> client.api._url('/_ping')
'http+docker://localhost/v1.35/_ping'
>>> client.ping()
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/arnold/.local/lib/python3.8/site-packages/docker/api/client.py", line 261, in _raise_for_status
    response.raise_for_status()
  File "/home/arnold/.local/lib/python3.8/site-packages/requests/models.py", line 941, in raise_for_status
    raise HTTPError(http_error_msg, response=self)
requests.exceptions.HTTPError: 404 Client Error: Not Found for url: http+docker://localhost/v1.35/_ping

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/home/arnold/.local/lib/python3.8/site-packages/docker/client.py", line 187, in ping
    return self.api.ping(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/home/arnold/.local/lib/python3.8/site-packages/docker/api/daemon.py", line 166, in ping
    return self._result(self._get(self._url('/_ping'))) == 'OK'
  File "/home/arnold/.local/lib/python3.8/site-packages/docker/api/client.py", line 267, in _result
    self._raise_for_status(response)
  File "/home/arnold/.local/lib/python3.8/site-packages/docker/api/client.py", line 263, in _raise_for_status
    raise create_api_error_from_http_exception(e)
  File "/home/arnold/.local/lib/python3.8/site-packages/docker/errors.py", line 31, in create_api_error_from_http_exception
    raise cls(e, response=response, explanation=explanation)
docker.errors.NotFound: 404 Client Error: Not Found ("b'Not Found'") 
```

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Run
```
podman system service --time=0 &
curl --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock http://d/v1.35/_ping
```

**Describe the results you received:**
`Not Found`

**Describe the results you expected:**
`OK`, as is returned by `curl --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock http://d/_ping`

~**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**~

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.0.2
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.3
Built:        Thu Jan  1 09:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.15.0
  cgroupVersion: v2
  conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.18-1.fc32.x86_64

  cpus: 8

    version: "32"
  eventLogger: file
  hostname: xps-9300
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1

      size: 65536
    uidmap:

      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.7.7-200.fc32.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 1243295744
  memTotal: 16343982080
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.14.1-1.fc32.x86_64

    version: |-
      crun version 0.14.1

      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    exists: true
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: slirp4netns-1.1.4-1.fc32.x86_64
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.4
      commit: b66ffa8e262507e37fca689822d23430f3357fe8
      libslirp: 4.3.1
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 2
  swapFree: 8237608960
  swapTotal: 8241803264
  uptime: 207h 8m 14.25s (Approximately 8.62 days)

  search:
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
  - docker.io
store:
  configFile: /home/arnold/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 8
    paused: 0
    running: 1
    stopped: 7
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:
    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
      Package: fuse-overlayfs-1.1.2-1.fc32.x86_64
      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.9.1
        fuse-overlayfs: version 1.1.0
        FUSE library version 3.9.1
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
  graphRoot: /home/arnold/.local/share/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageStore:
    number: 40
  runRoot: /run/user/1000/containers
  volumePath: /home/arnold/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 1
  Built: 0
  BuiltTime: Thu Jan  1 09:00:00 1970
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.14.3
  OsArch: linux/amd64

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.0.2-1.fc32.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Physical
